aboutUs: For over 36 years, we’ve been on the forefront of developing mobile warehouse, manufacturing and supplying operation solutions.

products.name: Endpoint Cloud
products.description:
Endpoint Cloud is a modern, workflow-based warehouse and operations platform designed to eliminate complexity, reduce friction, and scale with your business. Instead of forcing customers into rigid feature tiers, Endpoint Cloud aligns directly with how operations actually run—across inbound, storage, outbound, production, and performance workflows.

Built for real-time execution and visibility, Endpoint Cloud connects your people, processes, and systems through a single, unified platform with all features included, no per-user or per-device licensing, and transparent pricing.

products.name: Endpoint Mobile Sales
products.description: Simplifies invoicing and sales order processes. Empowers sales teams with real-time inventory visibility to close deals faster and deliver exceptional customer experiences.

products.name: Endpoint Procurement
products.description: Optimizes organizational purchasing decisions and manages the entire procurement process. Eliminates excess inventory and ensures necessary materials arrive just in time.

products.name: Endpoint Warehouse
products.description: Revolutionizes warehouse management by providing teams with tools to pick, pack, and ship orders efficiently.

products.name: Endpoint Manufacturing
products.description: Enhances production efficiency and helps take control of manufacturing operations. Enables confident and speedy decision-making.

products.name: Endpoint Payments
products.description: Streamlines AP and AR payment processes for improved financial operations.

products.name: [NEW] Enterprise Inventory Solution
products.description: A full-featured, complex inventory solution that integrates with many platforms.

differentiators:
Real-time ERP integration – Every scan reads and writes back into Sage in real time, eliminating manual entry, delays, and disconnected processes.
Embedded and cloud deployment options – Endpoint offers both embedded solutions built natively inside Sage 100 and cloud solutions that support growth into Intacct and beyond.
Top-tier customer support – Endpoint’s customer-first culture emphasizes responsiveness, accountability, and fast help when issues arise.
Scalable platform for growth – The platform is designed to scale with customers through unified architecture, stronger integrations, and infrastructure built for expansion.
Deep ERP ecosystem integration – Endpoint is positioned around ERP-centric operational workflows, with strong integration across Sage, Intacct, Acumatica, and other ecosystems.
Workflow-driven operational execution – Products are built around real warehouse and manufacturing workflows, helping customers improve execution, adoption, and time-to-value.
Configurability without heavy custom development –

industries: Small to mid-sized distribution and manufacturing companies
Businesses frustrated with complex WMS pricing and limitations

competitors.name: Scanforce
competitors.howWeBeatThem: Endpoint Cloud goes beyond traditional scanning solutions like ScanForce by delivering a complete, workflow-driven operational platform. While ScanForce enhances Sage with barcode scanning and transaction efficiency, Endpoint Cloud manages the entire warehouse lifecycle in real time—from receiving and storage to picking, shipping, production, and analytics. Built for scalability, it eliminates per-user and per-device licensing, allowing operations to grow without added complexity or cost barriers. With advanced capabilities like wave picking, directed workflows, and real-time visibility included from day one, Endpoint Cloud empowers organizations to improve accuracy, increase throughput, and gain full control over their operations—all within a modern, cloud-native environment designed for long-term growth.

brandVoice.tone: Professional, trustworthy, and solution-focused with emphasis on partnership and results. Uses confident language about capabilities while maintaining approachable customer service focus.

caseStudies.title: Parman Energy Warehouse Implementation
caseStudies.content: Implemented Endpoint Warehouse to determine product variance for customer shipments. Achieved easy ERP integration with exceptional customer service and immediate support response.

caseStudies.title: Manufacturing Company SAGE ERP Integration
caseStudies.content: Streamlined inventory processes, reduced receiving time by days, enabled real-time inventory visibility across two facilities, and reduced overhead costs by cutting data entry.

caseStudies.title: Medical Devices Company Efficiency Improvement
caseStudies.content: Achieved dramatically reduced errors and increased efficiency, enabling more orders to be processed with fewer mistakes requiring fixes.

caseStudies.title: [NEW] Manufacturing Warehousing Automation
caseStudies.content: Added automation and control to warehousing processes without disrupting existing operations, delivering gradual transformation as requested by the client.

caseStudies.title: Parman Energy - Product Variance Management
caseStudies.content: Parman Energy implemented Endpoint Warehouse to find out the variance of products they can ship to customers. Easy to use, easy ERP integration, with excellent customer service and support team providing immediate help.

caseStudies.title: Manufacturing Company - SAGE ERP Integration
caseStudies.content: Mid-market manufacturing company streamlined inventory processes, made receiving to POs faster by days, gained real-time inventory visibility across two facilities, and reduced overhead costs by cutting data entry.

caseStudies.title: Medical Devices Company - Error Reduction
caseStudies.content: Small medical devices business dramatically reduced errors and increased efficiency, getting more orders out the door with fewer mistakes through the user-friendly software experience.

caseStudies.title: Manufacturing Automation Implementation
caseStudies.content: Small manufacturing company added automation and control to warehousing process without disrupting existing processes, taking gradual steps toward advanced functionality.

voice.guidelinesText: Clear, concise, conversational, smart, thoughtful, and insightful. Start with the main point of your message. Use contractions, sentence case, and end everything with punctuation. Consider real needs, and offer genuine help, empathy, and advice. When you have data to back up your point, use it.
voice.documentContent:
Brand Guidelines.

Version 1.3 / February 2025



Table of contents.











SECTION A:

Brand overview.





SECTION B:

Visual identity.

A1 | Company overview.	04

A2 | Brand strategy.	09















B1 | Brand Marks.	16

B2 | Typography.	23

B3 | Color.	28

B4 | Imagery.	32























02 — Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025













SECTION A:

Brand overview.









 03













A1:

Company overview.





“Start by knowing what you want and who you are, build credibility around it and deliver it online in a compelling way.”







- KRISTA NEHER



































 04



A1.1 - Who we are.











The Endpoint Automation Solutions Brand Guidelines serve as a comprehensive re-source to ensure consistency and clarity in how the Endpoint brand is presented across all touchpoints.





These guidelines provide a framework for maintaining a cohesive visual identity, tone of voice, and messaging that reflects our commitment to innovation and excellence in ERP and automation

solutions. By adhering to these standards, we strengthen our brand’s impact, build trust with our audience, and communicate our values effectively. Whether you’re designing, writing, or strategizing, this document is your guide to embodying the Endpoint brand.

A strong, unified brand ensures that every interaction—whether with clients, partners, or employees—reinforces our mission and vision. These guidelines are designed to empower teams with the tools and knowledge to create aligned and professional communications that resonate with our audience. From logos and colors to messaging and imagery, each element plays a vital role in telling Endpoint’s story with authenticity and confidence.





















— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025



A1.2 - Mission, Vision, & Values.





















OUR MISSION:



Our purpose is to delight our customers and protect our partners through seamless, efficient, and simple automation solutions.

























































— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025



A1.2 - Mission, Vision, & Values.





















OUR VISION:



Become the heart of an operation by interfacing and integrating with each operational endpoint, creating a fully efficient environment of excellence.

























































— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025



A1.2 - Mission, Vision, & Values.





















OUR CORE VALUES:

Build enduring relationships.

We believe the best way to accomplish our goals is through collaboration. Open and honest

communication is key, and builds enduring relationships that make anything possible.



Be receptive.

New ideas can come from many places. Here, we strive to be adventurous and creative in thought and open-minded and receptive to the new ideas from any member of the team.



Pursue excellence.

We encourage our team members to reach beyond their skills to get to the next level. Our

hope is for everyone to achieve excellence in their position through growth and learning.



Have character & work ethic.

Being responsible is not the same as holding yourself accountable for ensuring the job is done right and taking the hit when it is not. Every member of our team is expected to

demonstrate a willingness not only to complete their own tasks but to follow projects and issues through to their conclusions, helping others on the team along the way.

















— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025







A2:

Brand strategy.



“If people believe they share values with a company, they will stay loyal to the brand.”





- HOWARD SCHULTZ



































 09



A2.1 - Elements of value.











Elements of value.

These values are the intersecting attributes that Endpoint offers customers with the elements of a brand that our target demographic values most.











Innovation.

We provide innovative capabilities

to our platform all the time.











Flexibility.

We listen to our users and enhance the capabilities of our platform to adjust to important needs.











Commitment.

We are committed to our partners

and end-users’ success. It drives us.

Responsiveness.

We responds promptly and are

always keen to help our customers.











Stability.

We’ve been around since the ‘80s,

and are here to stay. Count on it!

Expertise.

We are experts at this, and understand your pains, because we’ve lived it, too.

























— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025



A2.2 - The Golden Circle.



Our “Why”.



















We create and support software solutions for major ERP platforms to help mid-market manufacturing and distribution operations to organize & optimize every point in their supply chain and business.









What

SERVICE





The way we help operations improve efficiency is by boosting the capabilities of the platforms they already use, and getting them comfortable with them.













How

ATTRIBUTES



We believe fully efficient operations create the bandwidth for their people to achieve and perform at their full potential.













Why

BENEFIT

































— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.0 / Nov 2024



A2.3 - Brand messaging personality.











Brand Character.

How would people describe us if we were a person instead of a company?











“They’re a total Problem Solver.”

Yes, it’s true. We’re driven to find innovative solutions not just for our clients,

but for ourselves as well. It’s how we got our start!









“They’re definitely a People Person.”

Just because you’re great at what you do doesn’t mean you can’t be

friendly and empathetic. We believe in the golden rule.





“They’re such a Data Head.”

Computer nerds. Tech geeks. Guilty. We love getting the most we possibly

can out of our technology to make work easier.

























— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025



A2.3 - Brand messaging personality



Brand tone of voice.





How do we want to sound to people who read

“THEY’RE A TOTAL PROBLEM SOLVER.”



our written text, marketing, and articles?

Clear.

Start with the main point of your message.

Simplification helps solve problems.

Concise.

Don’t waste time, people who are focused on solutions make their point fast.







“THEY’RE DEFINITELY A PEOPLE PERSON.”



Conversational.

Use contractions, sentence case, and end everything with punctuation!





“THEY’RE SUCH A DATA HEAD.”

Smart.

Don’t dumb down terms your users know—converse like a co-worker.

Thoughtful.

Consider real needs, and offer genuine

help, empathy, and advice.









Insightful.

When you have data to back up your point,

use it. It’ll help reinforce your point.



















— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025



A2.3 - Brand messaging personality



Brand archetypes.





Brand archetypes are universal character profiles that represent specific personality traits, helping brands connect emotionally with their audience by embodying relatable, timeless identities. Sometimes, you end up with a brand being an Archetypal Mix, with 70% of one and 30% of another. Endpoint is one of those brands.

OUR ARCHETYPES:

The Sage.

A seeker of knowledge and understanding, The Sage is driven by information and expertise, and wants to share it with others who can use it.



The Hero.

On a mission to prove their worth, The Hero works hard at improvement and mastery to save the day.





OUR BRAND MESSAGING MIX:

Lead with The Sage (70%) in messaging.

Emphasize data-driven insights, expert solutions, and seamless integration within ERP platforms. This establishes trust and positions you as the wise advisor.



Subtly weave The Hero (30%) into storytelling.

Showcase customer success stories where Scanco’s solutions empowered users to overcome challenges and achieve more, and grow.



















— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025













SECTION B:

Visual Identity.









 015







B1:

Brand Marks.



“Logos are the graphic extension of the internal realities of a company.”





- SAUL BASS



































 016



B1.1 - Brand Marks









Primary Brand Mark.

There are various ways to communicate that Endpoint Automation Solutions (Endpoint) can interface and integrate across a multitude of diverse endpoints, positioning itself as the heart of an operation.

This direction pulls back to the 100,000 ft view, and says “We improve the potential of your operation and improve efficiency from A to Z.” To that end, by using a simple ”left-to-right” visual solution, viewers can immediately make the mental association of end to end.

An entirely custom typeface, the geometry of the Endpoint wordmark is stable, unique, and runs the gamut from conveying a full spectrum with the point to point aspect, but also leverage subtle half-serifs to indicate individual points along the journey.

Automation Solutions has been set in a Sentence case formatting to help maintain a balance of approachability and genuine heart.













— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025



B1.1 - Brand Marks









Brand Mark color variants.

In addition to the main color variant that is Red and Black for light backgrounds, there are 4 other variants for various applications.



Reverse - This variant with a Red wordmark and White modifier text is reserved exclusively for Black backgrounds to ensure that the Red really pops off the page.



NoRed - This variant has no Red elements at all, so it too is reserved for only one color of back-ground--Red.



Black - When you are designing for a black and white output, or need to overlay a brand mark on a light colored photo, use this. It should always be a last resort.



White - Use this variant on medium to dark

backgrounds that aren’t Black or Red.







— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025



B1.1 - Brand Marks









Brand Mark variants.

The Endpoint Brand Mark has various permutations in which it can be seen:



Stack - This is the version that will likely be used on external facing materials that aren’t the web-site. If the Brand Mark will be in a centered layout or on its own (like on a mug or shirt), use this.



Banner - Since left aligned text is our preference for layout design, this version is likely to be used for better synergy in more text-heavy designs. It’s also perfect for really wide layouts that don’t have a lot of vertical space.



Wordmark - Limit this to layouts where the viewer has already seen 1 or 2 recently. If you have a PDF download on your site, that PDF could use this version, because they were already on the site. Also for less “corporate” swag and internal docs.



Bug / Circle - Only use these on less “corporate” swag items, and in conjunction with a full Brand Mark or name (ie. Profile picture on Social Media).





“Stack.”



















“Banner.”



















“Wordmark.”	“Bug.”

















“Circle.”



















Most preferred.	Second most preferred.	Least preferred.





— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025



B1.1 - Brand Marks









Sub-Brand Marks.

Each of our sub-branded lines of solutions have

a unique mark and color palette. This is to clearly indicate when a viewer is observing a layout that is specifically dedicated to information or promotion of that particular offering.



These marks can be used all together on generically branded layouts to demonstrate our full end-

to-end capability, or used on their own uniquely treated materials.



For example, if we had a battlecard specifically related to Endpoint Payments, our Red would only be used for links, while the rest of the color palette would replace instances of Red tones with the Endpoint Payments blue palette.



Whenever a single one of these marks is used in a layout, it should have the Stack or Banner brand mark somewhere nearby for full context (such as a small version near the bottom of the first page.)











— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025



B1.2 - Brand Mark treatment



Clear space and minimum sizing.

When placing any of our Brand Marks in a design layout, please ensure that there is a minimum amount of space between the edges of the brand mark and any other design element, including the edges of the page.



The minimum amount of space is determined by the height of the N in Endpoint. This will ensure that our brand mark is given its own area in which to shine.





The minimum sizing requirements for our Brand Marks are dependent on whether or not it has “Automation Solutions” in it. Text within a brand mark needs to be legible, so the combination marks have a larger minimum sizing requirement than the wordmark and bug variations.

Minimum Size: 1.5” wide or 110px wide	Minimum Size: 0.75” wide or 55px wide









Minimum Size: 2.5” wide or 200px wide	Minimum Size: 0.2” wide or 15px wide







— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025



B1.2 - Brand Mark treatment











“Please, don’t.”

You might be surprised the ways in which we’ve seen people misuse corporate logos and brand-marks. This list doesn’t just apply to our primary brandmarks, but to all of our sub-brand and suite logos as well.



So, even if you think what you’re about to do looks cool, please, please, please...















...don’t stretch, squash, or rotate it!



































...don’t add a drop shadow or glow to it.















...don’t modify its coloring.































...don’t alter the positioning or size of individual elements within the Brand Mark.





























...don’t apply a gradient to it.

...don’t forget about the minimum sizing and clear space requirements!









— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025







B2:

Typography.





“Your choice of typeface is as important as what you do with it.”





- BONNIE SIEGLER



































 023



B2.1 - Typography - headings









Headings.



Typeface Overview:

By blending iconic British and French designs with modern trends and tools, it aims to traverse a complex typographical and emotional landscape. Great for print and screen, this complexity is perfect to demonstrate a comprehensive service offering that can connect to a myriad of datapoints and marry it all together into one cohesive system for the betterment of the organization.





Recommended Use:

Typeset all headings in sentence case, ensuring the sentence ends with punctuation.

The largest “feature heading” (only 1 per page) can be set in ExtraLight (100) font-weight, but all other headings should be set in Medium (500) font-weight. 0 letter-spacing, and 1.1 em line-height (where the leading or line-height is 1.1x the font-size.





Feature Headings

















Bricolage Grotesque ExtraLight

Aa

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ

abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz 0123456789!@#$%^&*()?





Secondary Headings

Aa













Bricolage Grotesque Medium

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ

abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz 0123456789!@#$%^&*()?









— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025



B2.2 - Typography - paragraphs & subheadings









Body copy & Subheadings.

Typeface Overview:

A beautiful geometric sans, this typeface is so mathematically simple that it services as the per-fect complement to Bricolage Grotesque’s detail. It’s minimalism helps it convey the other side of the business, the friendly and helpful side, without beating you over the head with it.





Recommended Use:

Typeset all subheadings (distinguished from “Secondary Headings” as a heading that is used directly above or below the headings in Bricolage Grotesque) in ALL CAPS and colored in Red. The tracking/letterspacing for these subheadings should be 30pts.

Paragraph text, or body copy, should be set in Medium font-weight, sentence case with 0

letterspacing, and 1.5em leading or line-height.







Paragraph Text

















Outfit Medium

Aa

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ

abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz 0123456789!@#$%^&*()?



Subheadings

Aa

















OUTFIT BLACK

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ

abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz 0123456789!@#$%^&*()?















— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025



B2.2 - Typography - paragraphs & subheadings









Snippets & Numbers.

Typeface Overview:

A monospace font designed for printed code list-ings and the like, this “programmer font” designed primarily for use on the screen features attention to detail for high resolution rendering as well.





Recommended Use:

Occasionally, companies need to create documentation that features a lot of numbers that need to be carefully parsed (say, annual reports) or, as is the case with many tech and software companies, snippets of code to share, there is no better alternative than a monospace font.

For these rare circumstances, use Inconsolata Regular, and set it at sentence case with 0 letterspacing, and 1.5em leading/line-height.





Snippets and Numbers















Inconsolata Regular

Aa

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ

abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz 0123456789!@#$%^&*()?



















— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025



B2.3 - Typography - system font replacements





Fallback fonts.

We always want to use our brand-coordinated

typefaces whenever possible, but sometime it just isn’t for technical reasons! In those rare

situations, the following typefaces are acceptable replacements.



Headings.

The font family replacement for Bricolage Grotesque on emails and systems that cannot load non-system fonts is Arial. There is no system font that feels similar to Bricolage Grotesque, so the best we can achieve is compatibility on both Mac and PC.





Paragraph Text.

The font family replacement Outfit is Century Gothic on PC and Avenir on Mac. These are their most geometric font families, and should be a fairly seamless fallback on those platforms.

Heading Text

















Arial Regular

Aa

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ

abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz 0123456789!@#$%^&*()?





Paragraphs

Aa

















Century Gothic

ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ

abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz 0123456789!@#$%^&*()?















— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025







B3:

Color.







“Colors express the main psychic functions of man.”





- CARL JUNG



































 028



B3.1 - Main colors





Primary palette.

The primary palette should be used 90% of the time. Black, White, and Red are the key tones to use, with various Shades and Tints of these as backup.



Pantone Black 5 has more warmth than Process Black, so it’s a great option for headings, while the paragraph text is better set in Process Black.



Our sub-brands have unique color palettes to set them apart from one another. For our unique Enterprise sub-brand we tie it back to our core by using the three Pantone Black 5 C tints as its

unique color palette. However, these tints are still allowed for generic brand materials. Enterprise-specific layouts should just avoid using Process Black and Pantone 199 C.















PANTONE 199 C

RGB 228 / 34 / 38 HEX #E42226

These three swatches also double as

the Enterprise sub-brand palette.

B3.2 - Secondary colors









Secondary palette.

The Secondary Palette is full of additional Hues that are representative of the various subdivisions of Endpoint. In addition to use on context-sensitive layouts dedicated to these specific sub-brands, these secondary hues may also be used on graphics that need a little extra.



For example, a pie chart or infographic that needs to represent a wider variety of datapoints could leverage these additional colors.

B4:

Imagery.





“Products are made in a factory but brands are created in the mind.”





- WALTER LANDOR



































 032



B4.1 - Imagery



Photography.

When we use photography in our layouts, most of the time it should include people. Photos should be in the bright to warm spectrum, where whites appear without too many cool tones present.



Images should focus on the subjects and allow them to be the primary element of the photo that draws the eye. When selecting stock photography, try to represent our broad range of end users with as much human diversity as possible!



With the exception of staff shots (Endpoint staff

are always encouraged to smile in photos!), stock images of people should feel authentic above all else. One of the best ways to accomplish this with stock photography is by avoiding overuse of large smiles. Yes, colleagues can enjoy each other’s company, but when EVERY image seems like a funny situation, they lose authenticity in aggregate.



Expressions of neutral focus, respectful collaboration, and mild amusement will go a long way here.























— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025



B4.1 - Imagery



Things to avoid.

When selecting, shooting, or editing photography	1	2

for our layouts, please avoid images with:



Low saturation or black and white imagery.



Cool color grading.



Outlandish photo filters.

3	4

Light-based issues (ex. flash photography odd-

ities, extreme lense flares, harsh lighting that is

overly distracting).



People who are “overly stock-y” (ex. posing, smiling way too hard, laughing while pointing at a powerpoint slide, etc.).



Anachronisms or environmental oddities (ex.	5	6

phones or computers that are several decades old, smoking or drinking in a workplace, clothing from decades ago, etc.)

















— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025



B4.2 - Graphic elements



Iconography.











We use the FontAwesome series of icons for in-stances in our collateral and online presences where we want a little extra graphical help but don’t want to go overboard.



This series of icons is a perfect match for our Brand Mark, as it features both elements of sharpness

as well as elements with subtle rounded corners as well. Their simplicity works at very small sizes, and is a great match for our brand.



When using icons in your layouts, go to the archive  of icons, search for the perfect match, click it, and download the SVG file for recoloring within your vector software.





































— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025



B4.2 - Graphic elements











“End points”.

Representing a blend of classic “datapoints”, simplified circuitry, and a terminus point, the “End Point” is

a simple graphic element that can be used (only a maximum of once per layout to keep it from feeling overused. This page should be the only place more than one is every used on a single page.) to help bring a little break from text-heavy layouts, and provide a “high tech” feel to them.



As you can see to the right, there isn’t only one file for the End Point. These can be created by the designer in the software. The rules are simple:





The lines should be 1 px/pt wide, and the dots should be 5 px/pts wide. These can be scaled up as needed for larger formats, but the ratio of 5x wider than the line/ stroke is the hard and fast rule.



The line may have exactly zero or two 90-degree

“bends” in it, one clockwise, one counter-clockwise.



Those two bends should happen within a minimum distance of 2 “dot-widths” and a maximum of 5 “dot-widths”. The second, and final, bend should occur at 5 “dot-widths” or farther away from the dot to keep the dot itself away from “all the action”!



Min. distance from dot.







Min. distance for both “bends”.







No bends version















































































Max. distance for both “bends”.







— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025



B4.3 - Texture



Background pattern.

Our background pattern is a subtle way to add texture to a layout without being overt or even overbearing. The goal is not to draw attention, but simply provide a little visual interest.



Overlaid on a white background, the Black or Red patterns should never be greater than 3% opacity (as seen on each white page of this document).



Overlaid on a Red or Black background, the White pattern should be no greater than 3% opacity.

















































— Endpoint | Brand Guidelines — v1.3 / Feb 2025
